visual_attributes.morphology: large stone representation of a human head
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 1.17 to 3.4 meters in height, weighing 6 to 50 tons

domain_specific_visual.headdress_style: cloth or animal hide representation, sometimes with feathers or bird motifs
domain_specific_visual.earspools: large earspools inserted into ear lobes

functional_attributes.primary_function: representation of powerful individual Olmec rulers

domain_specific_functional.portraiture: individualized features suggesting portraits of specific rulers
domain_specific_functional.dynastic_identification: headdresses may indicate different dynasties or rulers

relationships.description: The Olmec civilization created these heads as part of their monumental art.
The first colossal head was discovered at Tres Zapotes.

contextual_attributes.origin: Gulf Coast of Mexico, specifically Veracruz and Tabasco
contextual_attributes.historical_period: Early Preclassic to Middle Preclassic period (1500–400 BC)
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbolizes the power and authority of Olmec rulers; part of the 'Mother Culture' of Mesoamerica
